title: The Lost Era Modpack
description: Designed for players of all skill levels to fully utilize modded content, be it Magic, Tech, and more.
body:
The Lost Era is designed to deliver the complete modded Minecraft experience without the need for intensive research and information overload that often accompanies it.

Our focus is to provide an uniquely tailored in-game learning experience for players interested in exploring mods and their features at their own pace enabling them to define their own gameplay experience.

It offers a one-size-fits-all experience with a versatile yet structured gameplay around Tech, Magic, and everything in between, catering to a wide range of preferences.

Despite its wealth of content, its low system requirements ensures accessibility for all players so no one is left out.

    •                  

    This modpack offers an extensive 3,600+ quests, designed to make both modded and vanilla content universally accessible.

    The questing system in this modpack lets players explore freely, progress in their own way, and play in their unique style, all while having a structured framework to guide and support them throughout their journey.

    This experience was especially crafted and designed with multiplayer in mind where even players of opposing preferences can play together without compromise.

    Additional progression trees were also especially curated in an effort to explore non-conventional gameplay styles. 

    Finishing any quest chapter will give you a deep understanding of everything a mod can offer, empowering you to unleash your full potential in any project you set your mind to.

    As you advance in the game, these chapters will gradually unlock, ensuring you have a solid grip on the basics before introducing more complex information.

    Our modpack adheres to the concept that every playstyle offers something unique, each with their own specialties.

    We've introduced balance through custom recipes, configurations, and ore generation modifications, without resorting to unnecessarily difficult or convoluted mechanics.

    This ultimately eliminates overpowered mechanics, inviting you to embrace a fresh and balanced experience where you have the freedom to craft your own unique adventure without drawbacks.

    Each category will brings something unique and valuable to the table with distinct strengths, weaknesses and radically different playstyles.

    One of the aspects that have led to the success of Minecraft has been its widespread accessibility to virtually any system.
    To remain true to its philosophy many months were spent fine-tuning the performance of the pack for the optimal performance-to-content ratio, ensuring compatibility to as many computers as possible.

    As such, every playstyle has been fully fleshed out with minimal to no overlapping content, greatly reducing the feature bloat you would usually find in the average kitchen sink packs that would diminish your performance.

    Minimum Requirements:

    Requires Java 8

    Whether you're gaming on a state-of-the-art computer or your grandma's IBM Personal Computer, everyone can play together with no restrictions.

    In the base game, the survival aspect is incredibly safe with no real dangers outside of the earlygame, making much of the modded content overkill in nature.

    This has been remedied by building upon the original survival Minecraft experience with a focus on making those once banal mechanics relevant once more while not adding complexity to the overall experience.

    Aspects of the game such as food, shelter and a way to defend yourself are no longer trivial, exploring caves will be much more ominous with a diversity of enemies while dungeons are a whole lot more menacing that require some healthy precaution.

    The game will continue to maintain a healthy amount of challenge so players can fully utilize and appreciate the quality of life the once overlooked mid and lategame content modded Minecraft provides.

    Special thanks to:

    • SeraphSanshiro - for supporting me over the past 2 years and providing the eye candy designs to go with it. 
    • BamBam01 - for fixing and creating so many of the much needed tailor-made mods for the pack.
    • Swedish Kaito - for his huge achievements in revealing exploits and bugs.
    • PoWoKe - for baby-proofing the pack and greatly improving many QoL features.
    • TCLProject - for contributing to the magical branch of the quest system and identifying major crashes.

    ...and everyone who has contributed to the closed beta helping me refining the experience.
